message.content: {"items":[{"title":"German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) Pre-election Cross Section 2021 (GLES 2021)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13891","justification":"Covers political behaviour and attitudes, electoral participation, party preferences, and evaluations of government and political institutions in Germany in 2021, fitting the 2020s and the listed political topics."},{"title":"German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) Rolling Cross-Section Campaign Panel 2021 (GLES 2021)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13892","justification":"Focuses on political behaviour and attitudes during the 2021 Bundestag election campaign in Germany, including assessments of parties, candidates, and government performance, clearly within the 2020s timeframe."},{"title":"Political Attitudes, Political Participation and Voter Mobilisation in Germany 2021","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14121","justification":"Includes detailed measures of political attitudes, trust in political institutions and organisations, and various forms of political participation in Germany in 2021, matching political behaviour and organisations in the 2020s."},{"title":"German Internet Panel (GIP) – Wave 52 (2020)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13591","justification":"Part of a longitudinal panel on political attitudes and behaviour in Germany; wave 52 fielded in 2020 includes opinions on government, democracy, and political organisations, squarely within the 2020s."},{"title":"German Internet Panel (GIP) – Wave 58 (2021)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14025","justification":"Covers 2021 political attitudes and evaluations of government and political institutions in Germany, fitting the category of political behaviour and attitudes in the 2020s."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: Role of Government V – ISSP 2016 (German Module, released 2020)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13824","justification":"Though fielded earlier, the German release and harmonised dataset at GESIS in 2020 provide detailed German data on perceptions of government responsibilities, political institutions, and trust, aligning with government and political systems in Germany as available in the 2020s."},{"title":"European Social Survey 10 (2020) – Germany","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14019","justification":"ESS Round 10 includes a German sample with extensive measures of political trust, participation, and attitudes towards government and political institutions, with fieldwork in 2020 in Germany."},{"title":"European Social Survey 11 (2022) – Germany","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14116","justification":"ESS Round 11 provides 2022 German data on political trust, satisfaction with democracy, political participation, and attitudes toward institutions, matching the requested political themes in the 2020s."},{"title":"GLES Cross-Section Campaign Panel 2021 – Short-Term Campaign Dynamics","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13903","justification":"Panel survey around the 2021 federal election in Germany that tracks short-term changes in vote intention, party evaluations, and attitudes towards government and political actors, directly addressing political behaviour and attitudes."},{"title":"GLES Young Voters Study 2021","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13904","justification":"Focuses on the political attitudes, participation, and electoral behaviour of young citizens in Germany during the 2021 Bundestag election, relating to government, political systems, and political behaviour in the 2020s."}]}
